mnemonics: CLT Central: Remember the Central Limit Theorem (CLT) is the Core concept that Lets you Trust the sample mean's distribution will be normal, regardless of the original population's shape, given a sufficiently large sample size.
keyPoints: The distribution of sample means will be approximately normal, even if the population distribution is not, due to the Central Limit Theorem.
The mean of the sample means will be equal to the population mean (μ).
The standard deviation of the sample means (standard error) is equal to the population standard deviation (σ) divided by the square root of the sample size (n): SE = σ/√n.
commonMistakes: Confusing the distribution of the sample data with the distribution of the sample means. The plot is of the means themselves, not the original data points.
Forgetting that the Central Limit Theorem applies to the mean and not other statistics, and requires a sufficiently large sample size (n > 30 is a common rule of thumb) to be a good approximation.
